decisive.name: 决断
decisive.subtitle: 机遇面前，你的身体先于大脑做出反应

A.name: 动
A.desc: 先上车再说。行动是消除不确定性的唯一方式。

B.name: 静
B.desc: 谋定而后动。没有想清楚之前，不动就是最好的行动。

base_questions.scenario: 周五下午四点，老板在工作群发：「东南亚项目缺人，下周一出发，大概率 007，没有调休没有加班费，也没有明确升职承诺。但这是公司目前唯一的海外增量。」

option_a.text: 心跳加速。条件烂，但「出去看看」比什么都重要

option_b.text: 面无表情。连饼都不画，去了就是白嫖

base_questions.scenario: 好友突然发消息：「下个月自驾川藏线，路线我都看好了，你去不去？」你手里有个刚交了一半的项目。

option_a.text: 半夜开始查年假余额，请假条先甩过去再说

option_b.text: 「下次一定。」手上的事没干完，心是飘不起来的

base_questions.scenario: 房东发消息说下个月涨租 800。你看了一眼银行卡余额，又看了一眼窗外。

option_a.text: 当天就开始刷房源。不管去哪，先动起来

option_b.text: 打开计算器，算一下涨租 vs 搬家成本，再做决定

base_questions.scenario: 你在一个行业活动上加了一个大佬微信。对方说「有空聊聊」。回到工位——

option_a.text: 当天下午就发消息约时间。热度只有三天，过期不候

option_b.text: 先不急。等自己想清楚要聊什么再约，不然浪费机会

base_questions.scenario: 凌晨一点刷手机，看到一张明天出发的特价机票，目的地是你一直想去的地方。价格只有平时的三分之一。

option_a.text: 手比脑子快，买完才想起来要请假的

option_b.text: 太冲动了。特价票不退不改，万一有事呢

pool_questions.scenario: 朋友拉你一起做一个副业项目，投入不大但需要每周挤 10 小时。TA 说「先干起来，边做边想清楚」。

option_a.text: 「行，先跑起来看。」你讨厌在岸上学游泳

option_b.text: 「让我想想。」10 小时不是小事，得先盘一下时间

pool_questions.scenario: 突然收到一笔意料之外的奖金，数额够你裸辞三个月。

option_a.text: 第一反应：辞职信的措辞都想好了

option_b.text: 第一反应：存起来。自由不是三个月的事，是三年的事

pool_questions.scenario: 领导找你谈话：「有个新业务方向，没人做过，你愿不愿意试试？」

option_a.text: 「没人做过」这四个字比什么都让你兴奋

option_b.text: 「没人做过」也意味着「没人兜底」。你先问清楚资源和支持

pool_questions.scenario: 你在社交平台上刷到一个很火的活动报名帖，名额有限，先到先得。还剩 3 个名额。

option_a.text: 不犹豫，先抢了再说。不想去可以取消

option_b.text: 先看看具体安排，跟日程冲突了怎么办

pool_questions.scenario: 搬家中。你面对一屋子东西，朋友说「不能用的就扔」。

option_a.text: 你确实这么干了。五个垃圾袋，二十分钟搞定

option_b.text: 每件东西你都能想起一个故事。扔东西对你来说比搬东西还累

pool_questions.scenario: 一个猎头突然打电话过来，对方公司你感兴趣，但你现在的工作刚干了半年。

option_a.text: 半年怎么了？又不是结婚。面了再说

option_b.text: 刚干半年就跳，简历不好看。先稳住

pool_questions.scenario: 朋友说「周末有个很好玩的体验活动，我帮你也报了名，到时候一起去。」但你这周已经连续加班五天了。

option_a.text: 去！加班五天更需要换个脑子

option_b.text: 饶了我吧。周末我只想躺着不动

pool_questions.scenario: 你在学习一个新技能（比如编程、设计、外语），看到一个大牛的课程正在打折，但你不确定自己能不能坚持。

option_a.text: 先买了。花了钱就有压力学

option_b.text: 先试免费的。确定能坚持再花钱

pool_questions.scenario: 你站在一个分叉路口——左边是熟悉的路，右边是一条你从没走过的、看起来很有意思的小路。天快黑了。

option_a.text: 走右边。没走过的路才是路

option_b.text: 走左边。天黑了，别冒险

pool_questions.scenario: 同事突然辞职去了大理开客栈。你在朋友圈看到 TA 晒的苍山洱海。

option_a.text: 你的手指已经在搜大理的房价了

option_b.text: 好看是好看。但你心里清楚，那不适合你——至少现在不适合

decisive.subtitle_en: Before opportunities, your body decides before your mind does.

A.desc_en: Get on the train first. Action is the only way to eliminate uncertainty.

B.desc_en: Plan before acting. Not moving is the best action until you've thought it through.

base_questions_en.scenario_en: At 4 PM Friday, the boss posts in the work group: 'The Southeast Asia project is short-staffed, departure Monday, likely 007, no overtime pay or time-off, no clear promotion promise. But it's the company's only overseas growth opportunity.'
base_questions_en.option_a_text_en: Heart races. Conditions are bad, but going out to see matters more than anything.
base_questions_en.option_b_text_en: 面无表情。连饼都不画，去了就是白嫖

base_questions_en.scenario_en: Friend suddenly messages: 'Next month I'm doing a self-drive trip to Sichuan-Tibet, I've planned the route. You in?' You have a project that's half done.
base_questions_en.option_a_text_en: Middle of the night starts checking annual leave balance, send the leave request first.
base_questions_en.option_b_text_en: 'Next time for sure.' Work is not finished, heart cannot wander.

base_questions_en.scenario_en: Landlord messages that rent goes up 800 next month. You glance at your bank balance, then look out the window.
base_questions_en.option_a_text_en: Start browsing listings that same day. No matter where, just start moving.
base_questions_en.option_b_text_en: Open calculator, calculate rent increase versus moving cost, then decide.

base_questions_en.scenario_en: You added a big shot's WeChat at an industry event. They said 'Let us chat when you have time.' Back at your desk—
base_questions_en.option_a_text_en: Message that afternoon to set a time. Hotness only lasts three days, after that it is gone.
base_questions_en.option_b_text_en: No rush. Wait until you figure out what to talk about before reaching out, otherwise you waste the opportunity.

base_questions_en.scenario_en: 1 AM, scrolling phone, you see a discounted flight departing tomorrow to a place you have always wanted to go. Price is one-third of normal.
base_questions_en.option_a_text_en: Hands faster than brain, bought it then remembered you need to request leave.
base_questions_en.option_b_text_en: Too impulsive. Discounted ticket no refund or change, what if something comes up?

pool_questions_en.scenario_en: Friend invites you to a side project, low investment but needs 10 hours per week. They say 'Start first, figure it out as we go.'
pool_questions_en.option_a_text_en: 'Okay, let us run and see.' You hate learning to swim on shore.
pool_questions_en.option_b_text_en: 'Let me think.' 10 hours is no small thing, need to plan the time first.

pool_questions_en.scenario_en: Suddenly receive an unexpected bonus, enough to quit cold turkey for three months.
pool_questions_en.option_a_text_en: First reaction: already thought up the wording for the resignation letter.
pool_questions_en.option_b_text_en: First reaction: save it. Freedom is not a three-month thing, it is a three-year thing.

pool_questions_en.scenario_en: Boss calls you in: 'There is a new business direction, no one has done it before. Want to try?'
pool_questions_en.option_a_text_en: The words 'no one has done this' excite you more than anything.
pool_questions_en.option_b_text_en: 'No one has done it' also means 'no one has your back.' You first ask about resources and support.

pool_questions_en.scenario_en: You see a trending event registration post on social media, limited spots, first-come-first-served. 3 spots left.
pool_questions_en.option_a_text_en: No hesitation, grab it first. If you do not want to go, you can cancel.
pool_questions_en.option_b_text_en: First check the details, what if it conflicts with your schedule?

pool_questions_en.scenario_en: Moving. You face a room full of stuff, friend says 'If you cannot use it, throw it out.'
pool_questions_en.option_a_text_en: You really did it. Five trash bags, twenty minutes done.
pool_questions_en.option_b_text_en: Every item reminds you of a story. Throwing things is more exhausting than moving for you.

pool_questions_en.scenario_en: A headhunter suddenly calls, the company interests you, but you have only been at your current job for half a year.
pool_questions_en.option_a_text_en: Half a year what does it matter? It is not marriage. Interview first.
pool_questions_en.option_b_text_en: Jumping after half a year, resume looks bad. Stay put first.

pool_questions_en.scenario_en: Friend says 'There is a fun experience event this weekend, I signed you up too, we can go together.' But you have worked overtime five days this week.
pool_questions_en.option_a_text_en: Go! Five days of overtime means you need to change your mind more.
pool_questions_en.option_b_text_en: Spare me. Weekend I just want to lie down and do nothing.

pool_questions_en.scenario_en: You are learning a new skill (coding, design, foreign language), see a discounted course from an expert, but unsure if you can stick with it.
pool_questions_en.option_a_text_en: Buy first. Spending money creates pressure to learn.
pool_questions_en.option_b_text_en: Try free first. Make sure you can stick with it before spending money.

pool_questions_en.scenario_en: You stand at a fork—left is the familiar road, right is a path you have never taken that looks interesting. It is getting dark.
pool_questions_en.option_a_text_en: Take right. A road not walked is not a road.
pool_questions_en.option_b_text_en: Take left. It is dark, do not take risks.

pool_questions_en.scenario_en: Colleague suddenly quit and went to Dali to run an inn. You see their Cangshan Mountain and Erhai Lake photos on Moments.
pool_questions_en.option_a_text_en: Your fingers are already searching Dali housing prices.
pool_questions_en.option_b_text_en: It is beautiful. But you know in your heart it is not for you—at least not now.

judgment.name: 判断
judgment.subtitle: 当你站在十字路口，拍板的那一刻靠什么

A.name: 明
A.desc: 数据、逻辑、推演。世界是可以被拆解和计算的。

B.name: 幽
B.desc: 直觉、感受、第六感。有些东西算不出来，但你知道。

base_questions.scenario: 两个 offer 摆在面前，薪资差不多，A 是大厂螺丝钉、B 是创业公司核心岗。你翻来覆去想了三天，最终让你「就它了」的那个瞬间——

option_a.text: 是你打开备忘录，把利弊逐条加权打完分之后

option_b.text: 是你洗完澡躺在床上，脑子里突然「咔嗒」一声

base_questions.scenario: 凌晨两点失眠，脑子里转的不是具体的事，而是「我这辈子到底要怎么活」。辗转到最后让你闭眼的念头，更接近——

option_a.text: 「想不清。但至少得先把三年内的路画出来，走一步算一步」

option_b.text: 「活着这事儿吧，越想越糊涂。走着走着就知道了」

base_questions.scenario: 你第一次见一个人，聊了半小时。回家后家人问你「这人怎么样」，你的回答更接近——

option_a.text: 「TA 说了 X、Y、Z 这几件事，从这些来看……」

option_b.text: 「说不上来。感觉还行/感觉不太对」

base_questions.scenario: 你要买一个贵重物品（电脑/手机/车），两个选项参数接近，你最终的选择依据是——

option_a.text: 详细对比参数、评测、长期使用成本。选了数据更优的

option_b.text: 参数差不多就行。去店里摸了一下，哪个「顺眼」选哪个

base_questions.scenario: 你在判断该不该信任一个刚认识的合作伙伴。TA 简历很漂亮，但你隐约觉得哪里不对。

option_a.text: 做背景调查。简历可以包装，数据不会说谎

option_b.text: 相信那个「隐约」。你的直觉从来没骗过你

pool_questions.scenario: 选餐厅吃饭，两个选项评分都是 4.5 分。你——

option_a.text: 点开差评区看 1-3 星的人说了什么。差评比好评信息量大

option_b.text: 看了一眼菜单图片。「就这家吧，看起来好吃」

pool_questions.scenario: 你在纠结要不要读个 MBA / 考个证 / 学门新技能。你的决策过程是——

option_a.text: 算 ROI：学费多少，毕业后预期薪资涨幅多少，几年回本

option_b.text: 问自己「我想不想学」。想学就学，不想学了就是浪费时间

pool_questions.scenario: 你看了一部电影，朋友问你「好看吗」。你的回答——

option_a.text: 「叙事结构有点散，第二幕的节奏……」你习惯分析

option_b.text: 「好看/不好看。就是觉得……」你说不出为什么，但感受是真的

pool_questions.scenario: 你需要决定要不要搬离现在的城市。你——

option_a.text: 列了一张表：房价、通勤、薪资中位数、行业发展……

option_b.text: 闭上眼想象自己在那个城市的生活。「嗯，能想象到」或「不行」

pool_questions.scenario: 团队讨论一个方案，数据说 A 好，但你的「直觉」说 B 好。你——

option_a.text: 跟着数据走。直觉可能只是偏见的另一个名字

option_b.text: 「数据只能告诉你过去，不能告诉你未来。」你坚持推 B

pool_questions.scenario: 你在书店/图书馆选书。面前有两本，主题差不多。你——

option_a.text: 翻目录、看参考文献、查作者学术背景

option_b.text: 随手翻几页。文字「对了味」就拿走

pool_questions.scenario: 朋友给你介绍了一个相亲对象，照片和条件都还行。你决定要不要见面的依据——

option_a.text: 看 TA 的朋友圈/社交动态。分析这人的生活方式和价值观

option_b.text: 见面聊了才知道。照片和信息都是二维的，人是三维的

pool_questions.scenario: 你的投资亏损了 20%。你——

option_a.text: 打开 K 线和财报，分析是基本面变化还是市场情绪

option_b.text: 盘感告诉你该割/该补。K 线解释不了恐惧和贪婪

pool_questions.scenario: 你在判断一段友谊是否值得继续。你的依据是——

option_a.text: 回顾过去一年的互动频率、对等程度、付出回报比

option_b.text: 和 TA 在一起时你是放松的还是紧绷的。身体知道答案

pool_questions.scenario: 有人问你「你觉得这辈子最重要的事是什么」。你的回答方式——

option_a.text: 你真的有一套逻辑框架。健康、关系、财富、意义，各有权重

option_b.text: 你说不清，但你能感觉到。有些日子是「对的」，有些日子是「空的」

judgment.subtitle_en: At the crossroads, what do you rely on the moment you decide?

A.desc_en: Data, logic, deduction. The world can be disassembled and calculated.

B.desc_en: Intuition, feeling, sixth sense. Some things can't be calculated, but you know.

base_questions_en.scenario_en: Two offers on the table, similar salary, A is a big company cog, B is core role at startup. You think for three days, the moment that makes you say 'that is the one'—
base_questions_en.option_a_text_en: It is after you open your notes, weigh and score each pro and con.
base_questions_en.option_b_text_en: It is after you shower and lie in bed, brain suddenly clicks.

base_questions_en.scenario_en: 2 AM, insomnia, not thinking about specifics but 'how should I actually live my life.' The thought that finally makes you close your eyes is closer to—
base_questions_en.option_a_text_en: Cannot figure it out. But at least need to map out the next three years, take it step by step.
base_questions_en.option_b_text_en: This living thing, the more you think the more confused. You will know as you go.

base_questions_en.scenario_en: You meet someone for the first time, chat for half an hour. At home family asks 'How was the person?' Your answer is closer to—
base_questions_en.option_a_text_en: They said X, Y, Z these things, based on these...
base_questions_en.option_b_text_en: Cannot quite say. Feels okay or feels not right.

base_questions_en.scenario_en: You are buying something expensive (computer/phone/car), two options have similar specs. Your final choice is based on—
base_questions_en.option_a_text_en: Compare specs, reviews, long-term cost in detail. Chose the one with better data.
base_questions_en.option_b_text_en: Specs are similar enough. Went to the store to touch it, whichever 'looks right' I choose.

base_questions_en.scenario_en: You are judging whether to trust a newly met partner. Their resume is impressive, but something feels off.
base_questions_en.option_a_text_en: Do background check. Resume can be packaged, data does not lie.
base_questions_en.option_b_text_en: Trust that 'gut feeling.' Your intuition has never lied to you.

pool_questions_en.scenario_en: Choosing a restaurant, both options are 4.5 rating. You—
pool_questions_en.option_a_text_en: Open the 1-3 star reviews to see what people said. Negative reviews have more info than positive.
pool_questions_en.option_b_text_en: Glanced at menu photos. 'This one, looks tasty.'

pool_questions_en.scenario_en: You are torn about getting an MBA / certification / learning a new skill. Your decision process is—
pool_questions_en.option_a_text_en: Calculate ROI: tuition cost, expected salary increase after graduation, years to break even.
pool_questions_en.option_b_text_en: Ask yourself 'Do I want to learn?' If yes, learn; if not, it is a waste of time.

pool_questions_en.scenario_en: You watched a movie, friend asks 'Was it good?' Your answer—
pool_questions_en.option_a_text_en: The narrative structure is a bit loose, the second act is rhythm... You habitually analyze.
pool_questions_en.option_b_text_en: Good or bad. Just feel... You cannot explain why, but the feeling is real.

pool_questions_en.scenario_en: You need to decide whether to leave your current city. You—
pool_questions_en.option_a_text_en: Made a table: housing price, commute, median salary, industry development...
pool_questions_en.option_b_text_en: Close eyes and imagine your life in that city. 'Yeah, can imagine' or 'No way.'

pool_questions_en.scenario_en: Team discusses a proposal, data says A is better, but your 'intuition' says B is better. You—
pool_questions_en.option_a_text_en: Follow the data. Intuition might just be another name for bias.
pool_questions_en.option_b_text_en: Data can only tell you the past, not the future.' You insist on pushing B.

pool_questions_en.scenario_en: Choosing books at a bookstore or library. Two books on similar topics. You—
pool_questions_en.option_a_text_en: Flip through table of contents, check references, research author has academic background.
pool_questions_en.option_b_text_en: Flip through a few pages. If the writing 'hits the spot,' take it.

pool_questions_en.scenario_en: Friend sets you up with someone, photo and conditions are okay. Your basis for deciding to meet—
pool_questions_en.option_a_text_en: Check their Moments or socials. Analyze this person has lifestyle and values.
pool_questions_en.option_b_text_en: Have to talk in person to know. Photos and info are 2D, people are 3D.

pool_questions_en.scenario_en: Your investment lost 20%. You—
pool_questions_en.option_a_text_en: Open charts and financial reports, analyze whether it is fundamental change or market sentiment.
pool_questions_en.option_b_text_en: Trading intuition tells you to cut or add. Charts cannot explain fear and greed.

pool_questions_en.scenario_en: You are judging whether a friendship is worth continuing. Your basis—
pool_questions_en.option_a_text_en: Review past year interaction frequency, reciprocity, input-output ratio.
pool_questions_en.option_b_text_en: When you are with them, are you relaxed or tense? Your body knows the answer.

pool_questions_en.scenario_en: Someone asks 'What do you think is the most important thing in life?' Your way of answering—
pool_questions_en.option_a_text_en: You really have a logical framework. Health, relationships, wealth, meaning, each has weight.
pool_questions_en.option_b_text_en: You cannot explain it, but you can feel it. Some days are 'right,' some days are 'empty.'

social.name: 处事
social.subtitle: 当世界推你一把，你推回去的方式

A.name: 刚
A.desc: 正面对峙。问题摆在台面上，比任何迂回都高效。

B.name: 柔
B.desc: 迂回渗透。风能吹到刀砍不到的地方。

base_questions.scenario: 跨部门评审会，对方 Leader 的方案有明显硬伤——上线必出事故。你是全场唯一看出来的人。二十双眼睛盯着你。

option_a.text: 当场举手：「这个方案在 XX 环节有风险，我建议……」

option_b.text: 没吱声。散会后找到对方：「哥，有个地方咱再聊聊？」

base_questions.scenario: 过年聚餐，一个远房长辈当着全家人的面问你：「一个月挣多少？什么时候买房？谈对象没？」一桌人安静了等你回答。

option_a.text: 笑着说：「挣得不多但够花。您家孩子呢？」把球打回去

option_b.text: 打哈哈糊弄过去，不跟 TA 一般见识，但心里记下了

base_questions.scenario: 一个同事老是把活推给你，今天又在群里@你，字里行间暗示是你的问题。

option_a.text: 群里直接@回去，把时间线和聊天记录摆出来

option_b.text: 截图存档。然后找个合适的时机，用合适的方式收拾 TA

base_questions.scenario: 你在餐厅吃饭，服务员端上来一道明显做错的菜。你——

option_a.text: 直接叫服务员：「这道菜不对，麻烦换一下」

option_b.text: 犹豫一下，心想算了，也不难吃，凑合吃吧

base_questions.scenario: 你在社交媒体发了一条内容，有人在评论区阴阳怪气地杠你。

option_a.text: 直接怼回去，或者把评论挂出来让大家评理

option_b.text: 无视/删除/拉黑。不值得为这种人消耗一分钟

pool_questions.scenario: 合租室友总是不洗碗。你已经忍了三次了。今天你又看到水槽里堆着的碗。

option_a.text: 直接敲门：「碗的事，咱得定个规矩」

option_b.text: 在冰箱上贴个委婉的便签，或者找个气氛好的时候随口提一下

pool_questions.scenario: 开会时领导说了一个你明确不同意的决定。会议还有十个人。

option_a.text: 当场举手表达不同意见。会议就是用来讨论的

option_b.text: 会后单独找领导聊。当众反对没有意义，还树敌

pool_questions.scenario: 朋友找你借钱。数额不小，但你不是特别确定 TA 能还。

option_a.text: 「不借。」直接说。借钱伤感情，不如一开始就拒绝

option_b.text: 找一个对方能接受的理由委婉拒绝。比如「最近手头也紧」

pool_questions.scenario: 有人在排队时插队插到你前面。

option_a.text: 「不好意思，队尾在后面。」你直接说

option_b.text: 瞪一眼，心里骂一句，但大概率不会开口

pool_questions.scenario: 你被领导当众批评了，但你觉得批评的内容不公平。

option_a.text: 当场解释/反驳。不公平的批评不能默认接受

option_b.text: 先忍着。会后找领导私下说清楚事实

pool_questions.scenario: 朋友做了一个你不太认可的决定（比如辞职去搞直播）。TA 兴致勃勃地告诉你。

option_a.text: 直接说你的担忧：「你确定吗？想清楚退路了吗？」

option_b.text: 「加油！」先支持。泼冷水的事等 TA 撞墙了再说

pool_questions.scenario: 你在团队里发现了一个流程问题，拖慢了所有人的效率。你——

option_a.text: 在群里直接指出问题，提议改进方案

option_b.text: 先跟关键利益相关者私下沟通，拿到支持再公开提

pool_questions.scenario: 有人在你面前说另一个朋友的坏话。

option_a.text: 「我不这么觉得。TA 其实……」直接反驳

option_b.text: 不接话、不附和、也不反驳。换个话题

pool_questions.scenario: 你买单时发现账单多算了钱。数额不大，就几十块。

option_a.text: 叫回来：「这账好像不太对。」该是多少就是多少

option_b.text: 算了，几十块的事。但下次不去这家了

pool_questions.scenario: 你发现身边一个朋友正在被骗（比如杀猪盘/传销）。TA 完全不信你的提醒。

option_a.text: 想尽一切办法阻止。报警也好、找 TA 家人也好，不能看着 TA 跳火坑

option_b.text: 已经提醒过了，成年人要为自己的选择负责。你只能做到这一步

social.subtitle_en: When the world pushes you, how you push back.

A.desc_en: Direct confrontation. Problems laid on the table beat any detour in efficiency.

B.desc_en: Indirect infiltration. Wind can reach where the sword cannot cut.

base_questions_en.scenario_en: Cross-department review, the other leader's proposal has obvious flaws—it will definitely cause issues if launched. You are the only one who sees it. Twenty pairs of eyes on you.
base_questions_en.option_a_text_en: Raise hand on the spot: 'This plan has risk at XX phase, I suggest...'
base_questions_en.option_b_text_en: Stay quiet. After meeting, find them: 'Bro, let us chat about something?'

base_questions_en.scenario_en: New Year dinner, a distant elder asks in front of everyone: 'How much do you earn monthly? When to buy a house? Got a partner?' The whole table waits for your answer.
base_questions_en.option_a_text_en: Smile: 'Not much but enough to get by. How about your kids?' Pass the ball back.
base_questions_en.option_b_text_en: Laugh it off, do not take it seriously, but note it in your heart.

base_questions_en.scenario_en: A colleague always pushes work to you, today @s you in the group, hinting it is your problem.
base_questions_en.option_a_text_en: @ them directly in group, lay out the timeline and chat records.
base_questions_en.option_b_text_en: Screenshot for archive. Then find the right time and way to deal with them.

base_questions_en.scenario_en: Eating at a restaurant, server brings a clearly wrong dish. You—
base_questions_en.option_a_text_en: Directly call waiter: 'This dish is wrong, please change it.'
base_questions_en.option_b_text_en: Hesitate, think forget it, not that bad, just eat it.

base_questions_en.scenario_en: You post something on social media, someone comments sarcastically to argue with you.
base_questions_en.option_a_text_en: Push back directly, or post the comment for everyone to judge.
base_questions_en.option_b_text_en: Ignore or delete or block. Not worth spending a minute on this kind of person.

pool_questions_en.scenario_en: Roommate never does dishes. You have endured it three times. Today you see dishes piled in the sink again.
pool_questions_en.option_a_text_en: Knock directly: 'About the dishes, we need rules.'
pool_questions_en.option_b_text_en: Put a gentle note on the fridge, or bring it up casually when the mood is right.

pool_questions_en.scenario_en: In a meeting, boss states a decision you clearly disagree with. Ten people in the meeting.
pool_questions_en.option_a_text_en: Raise hand to express disagreement on the spot. Meetings are for discussion.
pool_questions_en.option_b_text_en: Talk to boss privately after meeting. Public opposition is pointless and creates enemies.

pool_questions_en.scenario_en: Friend asks to borrow money. Not a small amount, but you are not sure they can pay back.
pool_questions_en.option_a_text_en: 'No.' Say directly. Lending money hurts relationships, better to refuse from the start.
pool_questions_en.option_b_text_en: Find an acceptable reason to politely refuse. Like 'Recent financial tightness too.'

pool_questions_en.scenario_en: Someone cuts in line in front of you.
pool_questions_en.option_a_text_en: 'Sorry, the back of the line is behind you.' You say directly.
pool_questions_en.option_b_text_en: Glare, curse internally, but likely will not speak up.

pool_questions_en.scenario_en: You are publicly criticized by your boss, but you think it is unfair.
pool_questions_en.option_a_text_en: Explain or rebut on the spot. Cannot silently accept unfair criticism.
pool_questions_en.option_b_text_en: Endure first. After meeting, privately clarify the facts with boss.

pool_questions_en.scenario_en: Friend makes a decision you do not fully approve of (like quitting to stream). They excitedly tell you.
pool_questions_en.option_a_text_en: Directly express your concerns: 'Are you sure? Thought through the exit plan?'
pool_questions_en.option_b_text_en: 'Go for it!' Support first. Pouring cold water can wait until they hit a wall.

pool_questions_en.scenario_en: You discover a process problem in the team that slows everyone down. You—
pool_questions_en.option_a_text_en: Point out the problem directly in the group, propose improvement plan.
pool_questions_en.option_b_text_en: First privately communicate with key stakeholders, get support before raising publicly.

pool_questions_en.scenario_en: Someone talks bad about another friend in front of you.
pool_questions_en.option_a_text_en: I do not think so. They actually...' Push back directly.
pool_questions_en.option_b_text_en: Do not engage, do not agree, do not refute. Change the subject.

pool_questions_en.scenario_en: When paying, you find the bill overcharged. Not much, just a few dozen.
pool_questions_en.option_a_text_en: Call back: 'This bill seems off.' Whatever it should be.
pool_questions_en.option_b_text_en: Forget it, just a few dozen. But will not come back to this place.

pool_questions_en.scenario_en: You discover a friend is being scammed (pig-butchering or pyramid scheme). They do not believe your warning.
pool_questions_en.option_a_text_en: Try every way to stop. Call police, contact their family, cannot watch them jump into the fire.
pool_questions_en.option_b_text_en: Already warned. Adults are responsible for their choices. This is all you can do.

attribution.name: 变通
attribution.subtitle: 当一切崩塌之后，你的手电筒照向哪里

A.name: 通
A.desc: 向外归因，向外寻找出路。此路不通就换路，不跟自己过不去。

B.name: 止
B.desc: 向内归因，向内寻找答案。所有的外部问题都是内部问题的镜像。

base_questions.scenario: 你主导了半年的项目突然被高层一句话砍掉。跟同事喝酒时，你说出最接近内心的一句话是——

option_a.text: 「方向没错，是这公司配不上。换个地方老子接着干」

option_b.text: 「说实话……XX 环节如果我当时多走一步，也许就不是这个结果」

base_questions.scenario: 某天深夜，你一个人坐在路边摊吃东西。工作看不到头，身边的人都在往前跑而你感觉自己在原地。你问自己「图什么」——

option_a.text: 「这段路是我自己选的，经历是我的。值不值不是今天算的」

option_b.text: 「也许我该早做选择。有些东西错过了就是错过了」

base_questions.scenario: 你面试了五轮，最后被拒了。HR 说「有了更合适的候选人」。你挂了电话——

option_a.text: 「他们的损失。」擦擦手打开下一个招聘 App

option_b.text: 回忆每一轮面试，「如果那一轮我多说一句……是不是结果不同」

base_questions.scenario: 你和在乎的人大吵了一架，对方摔门走了。你坐在原地——

option_a.text: 「TA 今天心情不好。等 TA 冷静了再聊。」你觉得问题不在你

option_b.text: 你开始回想自己说的每一句话，哪句重了，哪句不该说

base_questions.scenario: 你花了三个月准备一个考试/认证，结果差了两分没过。

option_a.text: 「题出偏了。」或者「评分标准有问题。」你倾向于找外部原因

option_b.text: 你翻开错题，一题一题看自己哪里没复习到位

pool_questions.scenario: 你努力了大半年，年终评优却给了别人。那个人的产出不如你。

option_a.text: 「TA 更会跟领导搞关系。」你觉得问题在评价体系

option_b.text: 「也许我应该多让领导看到我的工作。」你反思自己的曝光策略

pool_questions.scenario: 你的投资/理财亏了不少钱。

option_a.text: 「大环境不好。谁能想到……」

option_b.text: 「我当时为什么没控制住贪心。」你从自己的心理找原因

pool_questions.scenario: 你坚持健身/节食了三个月，体重一点没变。

option_a.text: 「体质问题。」或者「方法不适合我。」换一个方案

option_b.text: 翻开饮食记录逐天排查。「肯定有哪里我没做到位」

pool_questions.scenario: 你写了一篇文章/做了一个视频，花了很多心思，发出去没什么反响。

option_a.text: 「算法不给流量。内容本身没问题。」

option_b.text: 翻看数据：「标题不行？开头没抓住人？还是选题不对？」

pool_questions.scenario: 你帮了别人一个大忙，对方非但不感谢，还嫌你帮得不够。

option_a.text: 「这人白眼狼。以后再不帮了。」错在对方

option_b.text: 「也许我帮的方式不对。早知道先问清楚 TA 到底需要什么」

pool_questions.scenario: 你和朋友的创业项目失败了。复盘的时候，你——

option_a.text: 「市场时机不对。赛道没问题，下一波一定能起来」

option_b.text: 「我最大的问题是 XX。下次创业我一定改」

pool_questions.scenario: 你加班到凌晨两点，写完的代码第二天发现方向完全错了，要推翻重来。

option_a.text: 「需求方没说清楚。这不是我的锅。」

option_b.text: 「我为什么没有提前确认需求？太想当然了。」

pool_questions.scenario: 你回顾过去一年，觉得自己没有什么成长。你把原因归结为——

option_a.text: 环境。工作太忙、圈子太窄、没有好的学习资源

option_b.text: 自己。自律不够、目标不清晰、时间管理太差

pool_questions.scenario: 一个朋友突然疏远了你。你不清楚发生了什么。

option_a.text: 「TA 可能遇到自己的事了。」你不太往自己身上归因

option_b.text: 你反复回想最近几次互动：「是不是我哪句话让 TA 不舒服了？」

pool_questions.scenario: 你做了一个艰难的决定，后来证明是错的。回头看——

option_a.text: 「当时的信息只能做出那个决定。不后悔。」

option_b.text: 「我应该再谨慎一些。这个教训我要记住一辈子。」

attribution.subtitle_en: When everything collapses, where does your flashlight point?

A.desc_en: Look outward for causes, seek solutions outside. If this road is blocked, switch roads — don't fight yourself.

B.desc_en: Look inward for causes, seek answers within. All external problems are mirrors of internal ones.

base_questions_en.scenario_en: A project you led for half a year is suddenly killed by leadership. Drinking with colleagues, the words closest to your heart—
base_questions_en.option_a_text_en: Direction is right, this company does not deserve me. Find another place and keep going.
base_questions_en.option_b_text_en: To be honest... if I had done one more step at XX phase, maybe the result would be different.

base_questions_en.scenario_en: Late at night, sitting at a roadside stall eating. Work has no end in sight, everyone around you is moving forward while you feel stuck. You ask yourself 'What is the point'—
base_questions_en.option_a_text_en: I chose this path myself, the experience is mine. Whether it is worth it is not calculated today.
base_questions_en.option_b_text_en: Maybe I should have made the choice earlier. Some things, once missed, are missed.

base_questions_en.scenario_en: You did five interview rounds, finally rejected. HR says 'We found a more suitable candidate.' You hang up—
base_questions_en.option_a_text_en: Their loss.' Wipe hands and open next recruitment app.
base_questions_en.option_b_text_en: Recap each interview round, 'If I had said one more thing in that round, would it be different?'

base_questions_en.scenario_en: You had a big fight with someone you care about, they slammed the door and left. You sit there—
base_questions_en.option_a_text_en: They are in a bad mood today. Wait until they cool down to talk.' You think the problem is not you.
base_questions_en.option_b_text_en: You start recalling every word you said, which was too much, which should not have been said.

base_questions_en.scenario_en: You spent three months preparing for an exam or certification, failed by two points.
base_questions_en.option_a_text_en: Questions were off.' Or 'Scoring criteria has issues.' You tend to find external reasons.
base_questions_en.option_b_text_en: You open the wrong answers, check question by question where you did not review enough.

pool_questions_en.scenario_en: You worked hard for over half a year, but year-end award went to someone else. Their output is less than yours.
pool_questions_en.option_a_text_en: They are better at schmoozing with leadership.' You think the problem is the evaluation system.
pool_questions_en.option_b_text_en: Maybe I should let leadership see my work more.' You reflect on your visibility strategy.

pool_questions_en.scenario_en: Your investment or financials lost a lot of money.
pool_questions_en.option_a_text_en: The macro environment is bad. Who could have predicted...
pool_questions_en.option_b_text_en: Why did not I control my greed?' You look for reasons in your own psychology.

pool_questions_en.scenario_en: You stuck to fitness or dieting for three months, weight did not change at all.
pool_questions_en.option_a_text_en: Body type issue.' Or 'Method does not suit me.' Change the plan.
pool_questions_en.option_b_text_en: Open diet records and check day by day. 'There must be somewhere I did not do right.'

pool_questions_en.scenario_en: You wrote an article or made a video with a lot of effort, posted it with little response.
pool_questions_en.option_a_text_en: Algorithm not giving traffic. Content itself is fine.
pool_questions_en.option_b_text_en: Check data: 'Title not catching? Opening did not hook people? Or topic wrong?'

pool_questions_en.scenario_en: You helped someone big, they do not thank you, instead complain you did not help enough.
pool_questions_en.option_a_text_en: This person is ungrateful. Never help again.' The fault is them.
pool_questions_en.option_b_text_en: Maybe my way of helping was wrong. Should have asked what they actually needed first.

pool_questions_en.scenario_en: You and your friend's startup failed. In the post-mortem, you—
pool_questions_en.option_a_text_en: Market timing was wrong. The track is fine, the next wave will definitely rise.
pool_questions_en.option_b_text_en: My biggest problem is XX. Next time I start up, I will definitely fix it.

pool_questions_en.scenario_en: You worked overtime until 2 AM, next day discover the code direction is completely wrong, need to start over.
pool_questions_en.option_a_text_en: The requirements were not clear. That is not my fault.'
pool_questions_en.option_b_text_en: 「我为什么没有提前确认需求？太想当然了。」

pool_questions_en.scenario_en: Looking back at the past year, feel you did not grow. You attribute the reason to—
pool_questions_en.option_a_text_en: Environment. Work too busy, circle too narrow, no good learning resources.
pool_questions_en.option_b_text_en: Self. Not enough discipline, unclear goals, terrible time management.

pool_questions_en.scenario_en: A friend suddenly distanced themselves. You do not know what happened.
pool_questions_en.option_a_text_en: They might have their own stuff going on.' You do not attribute it to yourself.
pool_questions_en.option_b_text_en: You repeatedly recall recent interactions: 'Was it something I said that made them uncomfortable?'

pool_questions_en.scenario_en: You made a difficult decision that later proved wrong. Looking back—
pool_questions_en.option_a_text_en: Based on the information then, that was the only decision. No regrets.
pool_questions_en.option_b_text_en: I should have been more careful. This lesson I will remember for life.
